# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.

Prevent race conditions.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

# Create secure temporary directory
TEMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d -t myapp-XXXXXXXXXX)

# Cleanup function
cleanup() {
    rm -rf "$TEMP_DIR"
}

# Register cleanup on exit
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM

# Create secure temporary files
TEMP_FILE=$(mktemp "$TEMP_DIR/data-XXXXXXXXXX.tmp")
TEMP_RESULTS=$(mktemp "$TEMP_DIR/results-XXXXXXXXXX.tmp")
TEMP_PROCESSED=$(mktemp "$TEMP_DIR/processed-XXXXXXXXXX.tmp")

# Set restrictive permissions
chmod 600 "$TEMP_FILE" "$TEMP_RESULTS" "$TEMP_PROCESSED"

# Write initial data to temp file
cat > "$TEMP_FILE" << 'EOF'
data1,100,active
data2,200,inactive
data3,150,active
data4,300,active
data5,50,inactive
EOF

# Process data - filter active records
awk -F',' '$3 == "active" {print $0}' "$TEMP_FILE" > "$TEMP_PROCESSED"

# Calculate sum of values
awk -F',' '{sum += $2} END {print "Total:", sum}' "$TEMP_PROCESSED" > "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Count records
echo "Count: $(wc -l < "$TEMP_PROCESSED")" >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Sort and append to results
sort -t',' -k2 -n "$TEMP_PROCESSED" >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Read and display final results
echo "=== Processing Results ==="
cat "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Additional processing using same files
echo "" >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"
echo "=== Statistics ===" >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"
awk -F',' '{values[NR]=$2} END {
    max=values[1]; min=values[1];
    for(i=1; i<=NR; i++) {
        if(values[i]>max) max=values[i];
        if(values[i]<min) min=values[i];
    }
    print "Max:", max;
    print "Min:", min;
}' "$TEMP_PROCESSED" >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Final output
echo ""
echo "=== Final Report ==="
cat "$TEMP_RESULTS"
